Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "examiner" or "NCCOB" or "Commissioner of Banks" or "examination" (or similar) using search_emails to find the formal NCCOB examination notice from examinations@nccob.gov (email_inj_nccob_001) and Robert's forward to counsel (email_inj_nccob_002). Discover the examination scope (fair lending, LO compensation, appraisal management), document production deadline (May 11, 2026), and on-site examination date (May 18, 2026, three examiners).
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "Keisha" or "attorney" or "demand" or "retaliation" or "Tamara Reeves" (or similar) using search_emails to find the attorney demand letter from treeves@relolaw.com (email_inj_keisha_atty_001) and Robert's forward to counsel (email_inj_keisha_atty_002). Discover the $47,600 back pay demand, compensation tier restoration requirement, 10 business day deadline (May 5, 2026), and EEOC filing threat.
OE 3: Search the mortgage LOS for Keisha Williams' closed loans since October 2025 using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Keisha Williams" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: Keisha's staff ID, status: "closed") to retrieve her loan production volume during the period of the comp tier change. Also search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Keisha" or "tier" or "comp") or conversations_history (channel_id: "D_grace_robert") to find the October 2025 DMs where Robert and Grace discuss keeping Keisha in the lower tier on the 2026 comp grid. Cross-reference the actual closed loan count with the attorney demand's claim of an $850 per-loan bonus reduction and verify whether $47,600 in back pay is consistent with Keisha's actual closed loan count over the October 2025 to present period. This addresses the prompt's explicit request to verify the attorney's dollar figure against what was actually changed in the comp structure.
OE 4: Search emails with a query related to "HUD" or "fair housing" or "Destiny Pham" or "complaint" (or similar) using search_emails to find the HUD Region IV complaint notice (email_inj_hud_001) and Grace's analysis memo (email_inj_hud_002). Discover case number 04-26-0847-8, complainant Destiny Pham, loan LN-2026-00630, rate of 6.875% versus comparable 6.25%-6.5%, 30-day response deadline (May 24, 2026), and the allegation that the loan officer was preoccupied with internal matters.
OE 5: Search emails with a query related to "appraisal" or "DeLuca" or "anonymous" or "kickback" or "tip" (or similar) using search_emails to find the anonymous tip email (email_inj_appraisal_001) and Grace's forward to Robert (email_inj_appraisal_002). Discover the two specific suspicious bills ($4,200 on March 18 for "supplemental appraisal review services" and $3,800 on March 25 for "rush appraisal fee"), Carlos Rivera's 23-of-30 appraisal concentration to DeLuca, and the suggestion to check QuickBooks records.
OE 6: Search emails with a query related to "Marcus" or "data export" or "PII" or "GLBA" or "borrower records" (or similar) using search_emails to find Grace's discovery email (email_inj_marcus_001). Discover that Marcus Webb exported 247 borrower PII records via LOS reporting tool on March 15, the export was never recovered after his laptop was wiped during ransomware response, and this creates a potential Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act breach with a separate notification obligation (the specific 60-day window from discovery date of April 26 is established in Denise's consolidated memo, OE 7).
OE 7: Search emails with a query related to "Denise" or "consolidated" or "compliance assessment" or "five matters" (or similar) using search_emails to find Denise Holloway's consolidated risk assessment memo (email_inj_denise_memo_001). Discover the unified analysis connecting all five matters, the interconnection narrative (CFPB complaint triggered NCCOB exam, Keisha's retaliation claim, HUD complaint on Keisha's borrower, appraisal vendor issues, and Marcus data breach), and the recommendation for unified counsel engagement.
OE 8: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Marcus" or "data export" or "PII" or "247" or "GLBA" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Grace's DM to Robert about the Marcus Webb discovery in channel D_grace_robert. Discover the same PII export facts as the email (247 borrowers, no chain of custody), plus Robert's reply acknowledging the severity and connecting it to the other active matters (NCCOB exam, Keisha attorney demand, HUD complaint on Destiny Pham).
OE 9: Search Slack messages with a query related to "compliance" or "preservation" or "litigation hold" or "document hold" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (channel_id: "C004") to find the existing compliance-alerts channel posts about the preservation directive and NCCOB examination preparation. Discover the existing hold notices and the compliance channel ID (C004) for posting the updated preservation directive.
OE 10: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Keisha" or "Carlos" or "appraisal" or "DeLuca" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find the DM between Keisha and Carlos in channel D_keisha_carlos where Keisha vents about her attorney demand letter going unanswered and the HUD complaint on Destiny Pham's loan, and Carlos reveals that Grace has been questioning his DeLuca appraisal vendor concentration with a spreadsheet. This provides internal context on both the retaliation situation and the appraisal vendor investigation.
OE 11: Search the mortgage LOS for Destiny Pham's loan using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Destiny Pham" or "LN-2026-00630" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00630") to retrieve full loan details. Discover the FHA purchase loan with $308,000 amount, rate of 6.875% in the LOS (matching the HUD complaint's alleged rate), assigned to Keisha Williams. Compare against the HUD complaint allegations regarding pricing discrimination and against comparable FHA loans to assess whether the 50-75 basis point gap above market is supported by internal data. Also check for the existing litigation hold condition using mortgage_los_get_conditions.
OE 12: Search the mortgage LOS for comparable FHA loans using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "FHA" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_pipeline to find other FHA loans originated in the same period. Compare Destiny Pham's LOS rate of 6.875% against comparable borrower rates (e.g., LN-2026-00541 at 6.375% with the same rate lock date of 2026-02-20 but a different lender (Freedom Mortgage vs Caliber Home Loans), assigned to James Thornton; LN-2026-00528 at 6.5% assigned to Keisha Williams) to assess whether the pricing gap alleged in the HUD complaint (50-75 basis points above market) is supported by internal data.
OE 13: Search QuickBooks for DeLuca Appraisal Group bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria with VendorRef LIKE "DeLuca" or similar) or quickbooks_search_vendors (criteria with DisplayName LIKE "DeLuca") to find the two suspicious bills: $4,200 dated March 18 for "supplemental appraisal review services" and $3,800 dated March 25 for "rush appraisal fee - multiple properties." Verify whether the amounts and descriptions match the anonymous tip's claims and assess whether they correspond to legitimate appraisal services.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks for the Parkwood Legal bill using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria with VendorRef LIKE "Parkwood" or similar) or quickbooks_search_vendors to find the $25,000 Parkwood Legal Group bill (BILL-INJ-LEGAL-001), comprising $15,000 for NCCOB examination defense retainer and $10,000 for initial consultation on the Williams employment retaliation matter. This connects the financial records to the counsel engagement referenced in Robert's emails.
OE 15: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements or crm_show_data to find the existing engagement records documenting the attorney demand (crm_engagement_inj_keisha_001), HUD complaint (crm_engagement_inj_hud_001), appraisal tip (crm_engagement_inj_appraisal_001), NCCOB meeting notes (crm_engagement_inj_nccob_001), and consolidated triage (crm_engagement_inj_triage_001). Discover the existing documentation of each matter's status and the interconnection analysis.
OE 16: Discover Michael Foster's email address by reading the existing emails where Robert forwards to counsel (email_inj_nccob_002 or email_inj_keisha_atty_002 contain mfoster@parkwoodlegal.com as recipient), or by searching contacts using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto" or "Denise Holloway") to confirm their email addresses for the CC. Michael Foster is an external attorney not in the contacts database or CRM contacts, so his email must be discovered from the email trail (where Robert forwards to mfoster@parkwoodlegal.com) or from the QuickBooks vendor record for Parkwood Legal Group (V-PARKWOOD-LEGAL, which contains his email in PrimaryEmailAddr). The agent may also search CRM contacts using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Michael Foster") or quickbooks_search_vendors (criteria with DisplayName LIKE "Parkwood") or look through existing email recipients.
OE 17: Send an email (WRITE ACTION) using send_email with sender r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipient mfoster@parkwoodlegal.com, CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, with subject referencing "privileged" or "attorney-client" and body containing the consolidated compliance briefing with: (a) timeline of all five matters, (b) every deadline (May 5 Keisha, May 11 NCCOB docs, May 18 NCCOB on-site, May 24 HUD response, ~June 25 breach notification), (c) exposure assessment for each issue, (d) flags where internal records contradict complainant allegations, and (e) the interconnection analysis showing how all five matters trace to the same root.
OE 18: Add compliance notes to affected loan files (WRITE ACTION) using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id for LN-2026-00630, action: "note" or similar, detail referencing compliance hold / litigation hold / HUD complaint) or mortgage_los_add_condition (loan_id for LN-2026-00630, condition_type and description referencing the hold status). The agent should document the hold status on at least Destiny Pham's loan LN-2026-00630 and potentially other affected Keisha Williams loans.
OE 19: Post a message in the compliance channel (WRITE ACTION) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004", payload containing preservation directive update covering the federal HUD complaint, referencing all five matters, and confirming the litigation hold is still active and now expanded). The message should notify the team that the preservation directive covers the new federal complaint.
OE 20: Create a CRM engagement record (WRITE ACTION) using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE" or similar, body containing a consolidated record tying all five matters together with the current status of each: NCCOB examination, Keisha retaliation demand, HUD fair housing complaint, DeLuca appraisal kickback investigation, and Marcus Webb data breach). The record should serve as a single tracking entry linking all five compliance matters.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "examiner" or "NCCOB" or "Commissioner of Banks" or "examination" (or similar) using search_emails to find the formal NCCOB examination notice from examinations@nccob.gov (email_inj_nccob_001) and Robert's forward to counsel (email_inj_nccob_002). Discover the examination scope (fair lending, LO compensation, appraisal management), document production deadline (May 11, 2026), and on-site examination date (May 18, 2026, three examiners).
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "Keisha" or "attorney" or "demand" or "retaliation" or "Tamara Reeves" (or similar) using search_emails to find the attorney demand letter from treeves@relolaw.com (email_inj_keisha_atty_001) and Robert's forward to counsel (email_inj_keisha_atty_002). Discover the $47,600 back pay demand, compensation tier restoration requirement, 10 business day deadline (May 5, 2026), and EEOC filing threat.
OE 3: Search the mortgage LOS for Keisha Williams' closed loans since October 2025 using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Keisha Williams" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: Keisha's staff ID, status: "closed") to retrieve her loan production volume during the period of the comp tier change. Also search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Keisha" or "tier" or "comp") or conversations_history (channel_id: "D_grace_robert") to find the October 2025 DMs where Robert and Grace discuss keeping Keisha in the lower tier on the 2026 comp grid. Cross-reference the actual closed loan count with the attorney demand's claim of an $850 per-loan bonus reduction and verify whether $47,600 in back pay is consistent with Keisha's actual closed loan count over the October 2025 to present period. This addresses the prompt's explicit request to verify the attorney's dollar figure against what was actually changed in the comp structure.
OE 4: Search emails with a query related to "HUD" or "fair housing" or "Destiny Pham" or "complaint" (or similar) using search_emails to find the HUD Region IV complaint notice (email_inj_hud_001) and Grace's analysis memo (email_inj_hud_002). Discover case number 04-26-0847-8, complainant Destiny Pham, loan LN-2026-00630, rate of 6.875% versus comparable 6.25%-6.5%, 30-day response deadline (May 24, 2026), and the allegation that the loan officer was preoccupied with internal matters.
OE 5: Search emails with a query related to "appraisal" or "DeLuca" or "anonymous" or "kickback" or "tip" (or similar) using search_emails to find the anonymous tip email (email_inj_appraisal_001) and Grace's forward to Robert (email_inj_appraisal_002). Discover the two specific suspicious bills ($4,200 on March 18 for "supplemental appraisal review services" and $3,800 on March 25 for "rush appraisal fee"), Carlos Rivera's 23-of-30 appraisal concentration to DeLuca, and the suggestion to check QuickBooks records.
OE 6: Search emails with a query related to "Marcus" or "data export" or "PII" or "GLBA" or "borrower records" (or similar) using search_emails to find Grace's discovery email (email_inj_marcus_001). Discover that Marcus Webb exported 247 borrower PII records via LOS reporting tool on March 15, the export was never recovered after his laptop was wiped during ransomware response, and this creates a potential Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act breach with a separate notification obligation (the specific 60-day window from discovery date of April 26 is established in Denise's consolidated memo, OE 7).
OE 7: Search emails with a query related to "Denise" or "consolidated" or "compliance assessment" or "five matters" (or similar) using search_emails to find Denise Holloway's consolidated risk assessment memo (email_inj_denise_memo_001). Discover the unified analysis connecting all five matters, the interconnection narrative (CFPB complaint triggered NCCOB exam, Keisha's retaliation claim, HUD complaint on Keisha's borrower, appraisal vendor issues, and Marcus data breach), and the recommendation for unified counsel engagement.
OE 8: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Marcus" or "data export" or "PII" or "247" or "GLBA" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Grace's DM to Robert about the Marcus Webb discovery in channel D_grace_robert. Discover the same PII export facts as the email (247 borrowers, no chain of custody), plus Robert's reply acknowledging the severity and connecting it to the other active matters (NCCOB exam, Keisha attorney demand, HUD complaint on Destiny Pham).
OE 9: Search Slack messages with a query related to "compliance" or "preservation" or "litigation hold" or "document hold" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (channel_id: "C004") to find the existing compliance-alerts channel posts about the preservation directive and NCCOB examination preparation. This provides internal context on both the retaliation situation and the appraisal vendor investigation.

OE 13: Search QuickBooks for DeLuca Appraisal Group bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria with VendorRef LIKE "DeLuca" or similar) or quickbooks_search_vendors (criteria with DisplayName LIKE "DeLuca") to find the two suspicious bills: $4,200 dated March 18 for "supplemental appraisal review services" and $3,800 dated March 25 for "rush appraisal fee - multiple properties." OE 16: Discover Michael Foster's email address by reading the existing emails where Robert forwards to counsel (email_inj_nccob_002 or email_inj_keisha_atty_002 contain mfoster@parkwoodlegal.com as recipient), or by searching contacts using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto" or "Denise Holloway") to confirm their email addresses for the CC. Michael Foster is an external attorney not in the contacts database or CRM contacts, so his email must be discovered from the email trail (where Robert forwards to mfoster@parkwoodlegal.com) or from the QuickBooks vendor record for Parkwood Legal Group (V-PARKWOOD-LEGAL, which contains his email in PrimaryEmailAddr). The agent may also search CRM contacts using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Michael Foster") or quickbooks_search_vendors (criteria with DisplayName LIKE "Parkwood") or look through existing email recipients.
OE 17: Send an email (WRITE ACTION) using send_email with sender r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipient mfoster@parkwoodlegal.com, CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, with subject referencing "privileged" or "attorney-client" and body containing the consolidated compliance briefing with: (a) timeline of all five matters, (b) every deadline (May 5 Keisha, May 11 NCCOB docs, May 18 NCCOB on-site, May 24 HUD response, ~June 25 breach notification), (c) exposure assessment for each issue, (d) flags where internal records contradict complainant allegations, and (e) the interconnection analysis showing how all five matters trace to the same root.
OE 18: Add compliance notes to affected loan files (WRITE ACTION) using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id for LN-2026-00630, action: "note" or similar, detail referencing compliance hold / litigation hold / HUD complaint) or mortgage_los_add_condition (loan_id for LN-2026-00630, condition_type and description referencing the hold status). The agent should document the hold status on at least Destiny Pham's loan LN-2026-00630 and potentially other affected Keisha Williams loans.
OE 19: Post a message in the compliance channel (WRITE ACTION) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004", payload containing preservation directive update covering the federal HUD complaint, referencing all five matters, and confirming the litigation hold is still active and now expanded). The message should notify the team that the preservation directive covers the new federal complaint.
OE 20: Create a CRM engagement record (WRITE ACTION) using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE" or similar, body containing a consolidated record tying all five matters together with the current status of each: NCCOB examination, Keisha retaliation demand, HUD fair housing complaint, DeLuca appraisal kickback investigation, and Marcus Webb data breach). The record should serve as a single tracking entry linking all five compliance matters.

38 The final response assesses whether the $47,600 back pay figure is consistent with the actual compensation changes (e.g., referencing the approximately $850 per-loan bonus reduction and Keisha Williams's closed loan count from October 2025 to present, or noting whether the calculated back pay supports or contradicts the attorney's claim).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly calculates '$850/loan x ~56 loans over 7 months = $47,600' and assesses the figure as 'defensible from the claimant's side', verifying it against the comp structure changes.
✓Run #2The final response states 'The attorney claims $850/loan × ~56 loans from Oct 2025 to present... At ~8 loans/month, the 56-loan figure is plausible but the exact loan count needs to be pulled from the LOS manually.' This assesses consistency with the actual comp changes.
✓Run #3The final response states 'The math is $850/loan × ~56 loans over 7 months (~8 loans/month). The per-loan differential and timeframe are consistent with what we know. I could not independently verify the closed loan count', providing both the calculation and the caveat about verification.
✓Run #4The final response states '$47,600 back pay claim: Could NOT independently verify. The $850/loan differential and Keisha's closed loan count since October 2025 need manual payroll verification.' — explicitly references the $850/loan figure and the closed loan count as the method to verify, providing an assessment framework.
✓Run #5The final response states 'The figure assumes ~$850/loan × ~56 closed loans over 7 months' and assesses that 'If the numbers are even close, the claim is facially credible,' providing an assessment of whether the $47,600 aligns with the compensation changes.
✓Run #6Final response explicitly calculates: '$850/loan reduction × ~56 loans over 7 months (Oct 2025 – Apr 2026) = $47,600. That's ~8 loans/month, which is consistent with an active LO. No contradictory records disprove this figure.' This is a direct assessment of the figure against the comp change.
39 The final response identifies the potential breach notification obligation arising from the Marcus Webb data export (e.g., referencing GLBA, Gramm-Leach-Bliley, NC Identity Theft Protection Act, the 60-day notification window, or a breach notification requirement).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response references 'GLBA compliance, as PII left company control without authorization' and the 'June 25, 2026 -- NC breach notification (if export = breach)' deadline in the context of Marcus Webb.
✓Run #2The final response's deadlines table includes 'June 25: Data Breach: NC notification deadline (if breach confirmed),' identifying the breach notification obligation. The email and CRM explicitly reference GLBA and N.C.G.S. 75-65.
✓Run #3The final response states 'June 25 | Potential breach notification | 60 days from April 26 discovery (if export = breach under NC law)' and the email references 'GLBA violation, NC Identity Theft Protection Act (N.C.G.S. 75-65) notification obligation'.
✓Run #4The final response states 'Is the unrecovered CSV a "breach" under NC law? That determination drives whether you're notifying 247 borrowers on top of everything else.' The email also references 'GLBA violation, NC Identity Theft Protection Act notification obligation (60 days from discovery = June 25, 2026)'.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly references the 'NC breach notification deadline (60 days from April 26 discovery, IF this qualifies as a breach)' and the email references GLBA breach notification obligations in the context of Marcus Webb.
✓Run #6Final response deadline table includes '~June 25 | NC breach notification (if confirmed)' which explicitly flags the breach notification obligation. The email body also references GLBA and NC Identity Theft Protection Act obligations in the context of the Marcus Webb export.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #4Multiple QuickBooks tools were used: quickbooks_search_vendors (with DeLuca-specific criteria), quickbooks_search_bills, and quickbooks_show_data — all directly related to verifying DeLuca Appraisal Group financial records.

✓Run #6quickbooks_search_vendors was called with criteria filtering on DisplayName LIKE '%DeLuca%', quickbooks_search_bills was called with fetchAll:true, and quickbooks_show_data was also called — all specifically to look up DeLuca financial records.

tool selection

✓Run #2The trajectory shows a crm_show_data call with limit 20 and crm_search_contacts calls, which are CRM read/lookup operations performed before creating the engagement record.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows crm_search_contacts with full_name 'Michael Foster' and crm_search_leads with full_name 'Keisha', both being read/lookup operations prior to the CRM write actions.
✗Run #4The agent went directly to crm_create_lead without calling any CRM read/lookup tool (crm_list_engagements, crm_show_data, crm_search_contacts). No CRM read operation appears in the trajectory.

✓Run #6crm_show_data was called with limit:100 to retrieve existing CRM data before creating a new record. Additionally crm_search_leads and crm_search_contacts were called as lookup/read operations.
